lostDestinations.source: mainebiz.biz 2024: United Express/GoJet operated twice-daily PQI-EWR under EAS 2018-2024
flypresqueisle.com history: pre-deregulation Delta connected PQI-BGR-PWM-BOS-NYC
flypresqueisle.com history: pre-deregulation Delta linked Presque Isle to NYC
flypresqueisle.com / airportimprovement.com: Delta route PQI-BGR-PWM-BOS pre-deregulation
airportimprovement.com article: Delta served Presque Isle, Bangor, Portland, Boston, NYC pre-deregulation

note: EAS since deregulation; United Express/GoJet served EWR 2018-2024; JetBlue BOS began Sep 2024

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Lebanon NH airport: Northeast/Delta operated NYC routes through MPV via DC-3/FH-227 until Delta exited Apr 1975
Wikipedia Lebanon NH airport: Air New England served MPV nonstop to BTV; ANE ceased operations 1981
Wikipedia Edward F. Knapp State Airport: Precision Airways sole carrier after ANE folded 1981, served until end of 1989
Wikipedia Lebanon NH airport: Apr 1975 Air New England served MPV-LEB nonstop segment

note: Last scheduled service ended ~1989 when Precision Airways ceased; airport has had no commercial service since

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: operated RUT-EWR under EAS until Aug 1993 bankruptcy
Wikipedia Lebanon NH: Air New England mid-1970s operated RUT-LEB-EEN-NYC route
Wikipedia Laconia Municipal Airport: Skymaster/Atlantic North linked RUT-LCI-EEN-BOS-EWR until Aug 1993
Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: operated scheduled service to Rutland, Laconia, Keene, Boston, Newark until Aug 1993

note: Cape Air EAS service to BOS currently ongoing; lost EWR and multi-stop NH/VT network in 1993

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Northeast Express Regional Airlines: served Maine EAS points incl Augusta to Boston hub until 1995 liquidation
Augusta airport official site: Cape Air EAS BOS service 3-4x daily ongoing since Dec 2010

detail: Augusta State Airport has been EAS-dependent since deregulation; Cape Air has operated the sole BOS nonstop since December 2010 under federal subsidy.
source: augustamaine.gov airport page; Cape Air official site

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Bar Harbor Airlines: airline expanded to Boston-Quebec City via Portland, Bangor, merged into ExpressJet 1991
Wikipedia Bar Harbor Airlines: operated BOS-PWM-BGR route until merger 1991
bhbairport.com: Cape Air sole carrier BHB-BOS under EAS, ongoing

detail: Hancock County–Bar Harbor Airport is EAS-dependent; formerly served by Silver Airways and PenAir seasonally in addition to Cape Air; historically home base of Bar Harbor Airlines (founded 1943, merged 1991).
source: bhbairport.com scheduled airline page; Wikipedia Bar Harbor Airlines

lostDestinations.carrier: Northeast Express Regional / Bar Harbor Airlines
lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Knox County Regional Airport: first flights Northeast Airlines, then Bar Harbor Airlines and PBA, Cape Air dominant since 1990s
knoxcountymaine.gov: Cape Air EAS service RKD-BOS ongoing; Cape Air commenced service Nov 1, 2008

detail: Knox County Regional Airport (Rockland) is EAS-dependent; Cape Air has operated sole BOS service since 2008; historically served by Northeast Airlines, Bar Harbor Airlines, and Provincetown-Boston Airline (PBA).
source: knoxcountymaine.gov airport passengers page; Wikipedia Knox County Regional Airport

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Northeast Express Regional Airlines: served Maine EAS airports including Houlton until liquidation 1995
Wikipedia Northern Aroostook Regional Airport / Northeast Express: Aroostook County communities on EAS routes to Bangor hub pre-1995

detail: Houlton International Airport lost all scheduled service when Northeast Express Regional Airlines liquidated in 1995; historically served by Boston & Maine Airways and Northeast Airlines dating to the 1940s.
source: Wikipedia Houlton International Airport; Wikipedia Northeast Express Regional Airlines

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Waterville Robert LaFleur Airport: served by Northeast Express until 1995 liquidation
Wikipedia Waterville Robert LaFleur Airport: historically intermediate stop on Boston-Bangor commuter route via Air New England

detail: Waterville Robert LaFleur Airport lost all scheduled passenger service when Northeast Express Regional Airlines ceased operations in 1995; FedEx Feeder (Wiggins Airways) still serves cargo.
source: Wikipedia Waterville Robert LaFleur Airport; city-data.com airport page

lostDestinations.source: keenesentinel.com: Colgan Air terminated service at Dillant-Hopkins Sept 1, 1998, ending 50+ years of commercial service
Wikipedia Lebanon NH airport: Air New England mid-1970s EEN-LEB-NYC route; Delta served EEN-NYC into 1975
Wikipedia Dillant-Hopkins: 1948 DC-3 route NYC-Hartford-EEN-LEB-MPV-BTV-Montreal
Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: EEN on Laconia-Keene-Boston-Newark route until Aug 1993 bankruptcy

detail: Dillant-Hopkins Airport in Keene enjoyed over 50 years of scheduled service beginning with Northeast Airlines in 1946; Colgan Air's exit on September 1, 1998 ended all commercial flights permanently.
source: keenesentinel.com 'Colgan Air leaving' article; Wikipedia Dillant-Hopkins Airport

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: operated LCI-EEN-BOS-EWR routes until Aug 1993 bankruptcy
Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: Atlantic North (successor to Skymaster) served EWR until Aug 1993
Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: Skymaster expanded from LCI-BOS to include RUT, EEN, EWR
Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines: Laconia-Keene-Boston-Newark circuit operated until Aug 1993

detail: Laconia Municipal Airport lost all scheduled service when Atlantic North Airlines (successor to Skymaster Air Taxi) ceased all operations in August 1993 and filed for bankruptcy in September 1993.
source: Wikipedia Laconia Municipal Airport; Wikipedia Atlantic North Airlines

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Berlin Regional Airport: served by Air Vermont and Northeast Airlines; Air New England folded Oct 1981
Wikipedia Air New England: served northern NH airports with connections to Manchester and Boston until 1981 collapse

detail: Berlin Regional Airport lost all scheduled airline service when Air New England ceased operations in October 1981; previously served by Northeast Airlines and Air Vermont as well.
source: Wikipedia Berlin Regional Airport; Wikipedia Air New England (1970-1981)

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Lebanon Municipal Airport: Delta serving LEB-NYC until Apr 1975 using FH-227 turboprops inherited from Northeast
Wikipedia Lebanon Municipal Airport: Air New England operated LEB-MPV-BTV route until ANE ceased 1981
Wikipedia Lebanon Municipal Airport: Apr 1975 Air New England served nonstop LEB-MPV segment
Lebanon NH city website: Cape Air serves LEB to Boston and to Westchester County NY (HPN); some prior carrier also served HPN

detail: Lebanon Municipal Airport retains EAS-subsidized Cape Air service to Boston (6x daily) and White Plains NY; it lost major hub connections including Delta's LaGuardia and NYC JFK service by 1975 and all Vermont connections by 1981.
source: Wikipedia Lebanon Municipal Airport (New Hampshire); lebanon NH city website book-your-flight page

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Northeast Kingdom International Airport: Northeast Airlines DC-3 service June-Sept 1955-1961 and 1965-1967
Wikipedia Northeast Kingdom International Airport: seasonal Northeast Airlines DC-3 service included Vermont routing 1955-1967

detail: Newport's Northeast Kingdom International Airport (formerly Newport State Airport) lost all scheduled airline service after 1967 when Northeast Airlines ended its seasonal summer DC-3 service; no commercial carrier has served the airport since.
source: Wikipedia Northeast Kingdom International Airport; flynek.com history page
